behavior_summary: Provides player interactions for a summoned/placed golem NPC: describing it when inspected, presenting a contextual command menu on use, and executing commands such as Follow, Stop, Move, Open (doors), Attack (assign target), Leave (self-destruct), and short vocalizations (Grunt).
GOLEM::look marks/flags the item as inspected and makes the golem emit a short identifying string ('golem ') to the inspecting process.

GOLEM::use builds a choice list (Item::ask), dispatches to multiple behaviors based on the player's selection, and uses engine services (pathfind, movement spawn, door toggles, setTarget, destroy) to carry out the commands.
Follow mode spawns a background follow-loop (GOLEM::03CC) that repeatedly checks distance to the leader/target and spawns single-step movement actions until cancelled or conditions change.

Move and Open use repeated small movement spawns (METHOD::091C and METHOD::15E7) and/or pathfind to approach a target, with Move implementing a retry/step loop and Open toggling door quality flags then spawning a door animation routine.
notable_control_flow: Event dispatch on Item::ask result: string comparison drives a multi-way branch covering Follow, Stop, Move, Open, Attack, Leave, Grunt, and Nothing.
Follow command spawns U8P_GOLEM::GOLEM::03CC (background process) and returns; Stop calls resetRef to cancel/clear follow references.

Move command resolves the player's process target, queries Item::getRange and Item::getDirToItem, then loops: spawn movement step (METHOD::091C) + follow-up (METHOD::15E7) and retry until arrival or retry limit; falls back to barking 'Ugh ' if move not viable.

Open command pathfinds to the target door, spawns short movement/animation then flips a door quality bit (read/write via Item::getQuality / Item::setQuality) and spawns DOOR_NS::08B4 to animate/apply the change.
Leave spawns a short action then calls Item::destroy to remove the golem from the world.
open_questions: Exact meanings and semantics of the specific byte arguments passed to METHOD::091C and METHOD::15E7 (timing, animation flags) are inferred but not explicit.
Precise semantics of the door quality bit(s) toggled during Open (which bit(s) encode open vs closed) are inferred from context but not definitively identified.
How the follow-loop (U8P_GOLEM::GOLEM::03CC) is reliably cancelled in all cases — Stop calls resetRef but the exact cancellation handshake with the spawned process is not fully specified.

Whether some menu branches are hidden or altered when the NPC is in combat (Npc::isInCombat) beyond the observed guard/early branch — menu differences are inferred from a combat check but not fully enumerated.
The exact info id(s) and 'process exclude' semantics set by GOLEM::look (and by the follow loop) are present in the bytecode but their higher-level gameplay flags/UX effects are only inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_GOLEM::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_GOLEM::look' (examine) event for the golem item: set item info/processing state and make the golem produce a short identifying bark ('golem ').
entry_conditions: Event 0 / fast-area 'U8P_GOLEM::look' trigger when a player or process inspects/looks at the golem item.

function_map.name: U8P_GOLEM::look
function_map.role: Examination handler: mark the item and present a short identification string to the viewer.

function_map.name: U8P_GOLEM::use
function_map.role: Primary interaction/menu handler for player 'use' on the golem; builds Item::ask menu and implements Follow/Stop/Move/Open/Attack/Leave/Grunt/Nothing behaviors.

function_map.name: U8P_GOLEM::03CC
function_map.role: Spawned background follow-loop implementing Follow behavior: periodically check range to leader/target and spawn step movement until cancelled.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the background follow behavior when the player selects 'Follow'.

external_calls.purpose: Make the golem emit/display the identifying string 'golem ' to the inspecting process.
Present the command menu (Follow, Stop, Move, Open, Attack, Leave, Grunt, Nothing) and obtain the player's selection.
Resolve the player's currently selected target id for Move/Open/Attack commands.
Guard or alter available commands/flow based on whether the golem is in combat.
Measure distance to a target to decide movement vs failure (used by Move and by the follow loop).
Obtain direction to target for spawning a single-step movement action (METHOD::091C).
Spawn a single-step movement/action in a given direction for the golem (part of Move/retry loop).
Spawn follow-up movement/animation or delay to complete a movement step; used after METHOD::091C and in other short actions like Leave/Open.
Request the golem to pathfind to a specified destination (used before Open to approach a door).
Check the type of the target to confirm it's door-like before performing Open logic.
Obtain a door object/reference from an id when performing door Open.
Read door quality flags to determine current open/closed state before toggling.
Write modified quality flags to toggle a door's open/closed bit(s).
Spawn the door-handling/animation routine after toggling door flags.
Remove/destroy the golem item when player selects 'Leave'.
Clear follower/reference state when player selects 'Stop'.
Assign the golem's combat target when player selects 'Attack' with a valid process target.
Emit short fallback vocalizations like 'Ugh ' for Grunt or when Move fails/declines to act.
Used in the follow-loop to measure distance to the leader/target and decide whether to spawn movement steps.
Get movement direction to the leader/target for spawning single-step movement.
Spawn single-step movement towards leader/target when range threshold exceeded.
Follow-up movement/animation spawn used periodically in the follow-loop.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides player interactions for a summoned/placed golem NPC: describing it when inspected, presenting a contextual command menu on use, and executing commands such as Follow, Stop, Move, Open (doors), Attack (assign target), Leave (self-destruct), and short vocalizations (Grunt).
